audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for John's question, Jesus' signs, wilderness prophet language, Elijah motif, generation critique, woes to cities, hidden/revealed wisdom, Father/Son knowing, and rest/yoke invitation.

verses.restored: When Jesus finished instructing his twelve disciples, he departed from there to teach and proclaim in their cities.
John, hearing in prison the works of the Messiah, sent through his disciples
and said to him, Are you the coming one, or should we look for another?
Jesus answered them, Go report to John what you hear and see:
the blind receive sight, the lame walk, those with skin disease are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead are raised, and the poor are given good news.
Blessed/flourishing is the one who is not caused to stumble by me.
As they were going away, Jesus began to speak to the crowds about John: What did you go out into the wilderness to behold? A reed shaken by wind?
But what did you go out to see? A person clothed in soft garments? Look, those wearing soft garments are in kings' houses.
But what did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I say to you, and more than a prophet.
This is the one about whom it has been written: Look, I send my messenger before your face, who will prepare your way before you.
Amen, I say to you: among those born of women, none has arisen greater than John the Baptist; yet the least in the reign of the heavens is greater than he.
From the days of John the Baptist until now, the reign of the heavens is forcefully pressing forward, and forceful ones seize/press into it.
For all the Prophets and the Law prophesied until John.
And if you are willing to receive it, he is Elijah who is to come.
The one having ears, let him hear.
To what shall I compare this generation? It is like children sitting in the marketplaces, calling to others,
We played the flute for you, and you did not dance; we lamented, and you did not mourn.
For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, He has an unclean spirit.
The Son of Humanity came eating and drinking, and they say, Look, a glutton and wine-drinker, friend of tax collectors and sinners. Yet wisdom is justified by her works.
Then he began to reproach the cities in which most of his powers had happened, because they did not turn/realign.
Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the powers that happened in you had happened in Tyre and Sidon, long ago they would have turned in sackcloth and ashes.
But I say to you: it will be more bearable for Tyre and Sidon in the day of judgment than for you.
And you, Capernaum, will you be lifted to heaven? You will go down to Hades. For if the powers that happened in you had happened in Sodom, it would have remained until today.
But I say to you: it will be more bearable for the land of Sodom in the day of judgment than for you.
At that time Jesus answered and said, I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because you hid these things from wise and understanding ones and revealed them to infants.
Yes, Father, because this became good pleasure before you.
All things have been handed over to me by my Father; no one fully knows the Son except the Father, nor does anyone fully know the Father except the Son and anyone to whom the Son wills to reveal him.
Come to me, all who labor and are burdened, and I will give you rest.
Take my yoke upon you and learn from me, because I am gentle and lowly in heart, and you will find rest for your souls/lives.
For my yoke is kind/useful, and my burden is light.
